results.title: It's wonderful being a girl
results.brief_description: Explains the menstrual process, emphasising the normalcy of menstruation. Discusses good health habits, and gives tips on good grooming and poise.

results.title: It's up to you: fire protection
results.brief_description: Smokey Bear, a cartoon figure, makes a direct appeal to everyone to help in preventing forest fires. He explains how fires have caused in one year the loss of enough timber to supply the building needs of a large city. He shows a family picnic party carefully putting out their camp fire, while the father grinds his match and pipe ash into the ground.

results.title: It's up to you
results.brief_description: A survey of supervisory problems which arise in business organisations. The importance of conscientiously developing qualities of executive leadership is illustrated by the story of a junior executive in merchandising who realises that his effectiveness as a leader depends upon his acceptance and application of sound practices in human relations.

results.title: It's the only body you've got
results.creator_credit: Roslyn Simms, National Heart Foundation of Australia and Paul Griffiths Presentations
results.brief_description: Deals with the relationship between diet and heart disease. Outlines the risk factors which contribute to coronary heart disease and advises on correct nutrition and diet to minimise these risks. Especially designed for secondary school students.

results.title: It's the little things that count
results.creator_credit: Bates Manufacturing Company and Caravel Films
results.brief_description: A motivational film presenting the problem of the retail salesman in reaching the buyer and gaining their confidence. Develops a technique in combatting the price argument by taking into consideration factors other thatn price only. Shows problems of the retail merchant in getting salesmen to conserve and plan their time. Illustrates with a number of articlea what is meant by ‘showmanship in selling’. Though not intended, this film presents an amusing stereotype of a 1940’s American saleman.

results.title: It's so nice to have a wolf around the house
results.creator_credit: Paul Fierlinger and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description: An animated film, based on a book by Harry Allard which tells of an old man and his ageing pets, who decides to advertise for a companion to take care of them and pep them all up. A wolf named Cuthbert Q. Devine answers the call, turning their little houseboat into a happier place - but not without some adventures along the way.

results.title: It's so easy
results.creator_credit: Donald Crombie, John Martin-Jones and Australian Commonwealth Film Unit
results.brief_description: Deals with the basic rules of safety at sea. Emphasises the need to be aware of the dangers. Always check gear, don’t overload, carry a radio for any warning of weather change, state destination before departing, and obey the rules of the “water road”. Outlines technique of survival swimming.

results.title: It's Ruth
results.brief_description: A biography of Ruth Cracknell, the renowned Australian actor whose career has spanned half a century. While her work encompasses all genres and mediums she is best known for her comic roles, especially that of Maggie Bear in the long running ABC television series ‘Mother and Son’. In this documentary, interviews with various associates and friends focus on the eccentric elements of her work and her ability to juxtapose the tragic and the comic in her performances. Excerpts from ‘Mother and Son’, Beckett’s play ‘Happy Days’, Elizabeth Jolley’s short story ‘Woman in a Lampshade, Oscar Wilde’s play ‘The Importance of Being Ernest’ and Neil Simon’s play ‘Lost in Yonkers’ are included.
